claim: Context engineering—providing the model with detailed specifications, design documents, mockups, asset indices, and technical architecture—is the most critical factor for successful vibe coding of 3D games with AI agents.
actionable: Before asking Claude Code to build a game, prepare: asset inventory files, game design documents (PRDs), technical design documents (TDDs), concept art mockups, and skill definitions to maximize context quality.
source_title: Here's How I Use Agent Skills in Claude Code for Game Dev (Full Tutorial)

claim: Agent skills should be continuously refined and iterated upon as you build more products, incorporating fixes, gotchas, and bug solutions discovered during development.
actionable: Document edge cases and fixes found in real builds; integrate them back into your custom skills to improve their effectiveness across future projects.
source_title: Here's How I Use Agent Skills in Claude Code for Game Dev (Full Tutorial)

claim: Using pre-built, cohesive art asset packs (GLTF format) is far superior to generating assets via AI or creating them procedurally; manually sourcing licensed assets with permissive licenses accelerates development.
actionable: For game dev projects, purchase or find CC0/permissive-licensed 3D asset packs (Sketchfab, itch.io) in GLTF format rather than generating or building assets from scratch.
source_title: Here's How I Use Agent Skills in Claude Code for Game Dev (Full Tutorial)

claim: Creating an assets.json index file that maps all available 3D models and their file paths dramatically improves AI performance when working with large asset libraries.
actionable: After organizing assets, use Claude Code with Opus to generate a comprehensive assets.json index categorizing all GLTF models by folder structure and path—this becomes the single source of truth for asset discovery.
source_title: Here's How I Use Agent Skills in Claude Code for Game Dev (Full Tutorial)

claim: Using tools like Nano Banana Pro (multimodal AI) to create concept mockups before the PRD provides visual grounding that significantly improves the final game design document quality.
actionable: Generate concept art mockups with Nano Banana Pro using asset previews and game idea descriptions, then feed these mockups into game design document generators to create context-rich PRDs.
source_title: Here's How I Use Agent Skills in Claude Code for Game Dev (Full Tutorial)

claim: Compacting conversations in Claude Code before major implementation phases refreshes the context window, allowing the model to allocate tokens more efficiently for code generation.
actionable: Monitor context percentage throughout long sessions; when dropping below 60%, manually compact the conversation to free up context budget before starting intensive implementation phases.
source_title: Here's How I Use Agent Skills in Claude Code for Game Dev (Full Tutorial)

claim: The Three.js builder skill successfully handles common 3D game development gotchas including coordinate system issues, animation state management, and correct model facing—major pain points in AI-generated game code.
actionable: When building 3D games with AI agents, use or create a specialized Three.js skill that encodes solutions for rotation axes, animation blending, and coordinate transformations to avoid common AI-generated bugs.
source_title: Here's How I Use Agent Skills in Claude Code for Game Dev (Full Tutorial)

claim: Games are never built in a single shot; a playable first version with core mechanics working correctly is the critical milestone, after which iterative improvements on level design, balance, and polish make massive differences.
actionable: Treat AI-generated game prototypes as the starting point, not the finish line; prioritize getting to a playable version with functioning mechanics, then iterate rapidly on specific issues identified during testing.
source_title: Here's How I Use Agent Skills in Claude Code for Game Dev (Full Tutorial)

claim: Placeholder assets (basic geometric shapes) that the AI generates allow full game testing and mechanic validation without waiting for final art, enabling parallel asset production.
actionable: Instruct Claude Code to generate placeholder geometry for untested assets; this unblocks game testing and balance iteration while final art assets are being sourced or refined.
source_title: Here's How I Use Agent Skills in Claude Code for Game Dev (Full Tutorial)

claim: Using the /plan command in Claude Code and saving the generated plan as a markdown file enables portability—you can transfer the plan to other coding agents (Cursor, Codex) if you hit usage limits on the primary tool.
actionable: After Claude Code generates a comprehensive implementation plan, export it to a markdown file; this becomes a portable blueprint you can reuse with different AI coding agents if needed.
source_title: Here's How I Use Agent Skills in Claude Code for Game Dev (Full Tutorial)
